description: Azure resource type to Draw.io stencil mappings. ALWAYS look up a resource type here before constructing any icon path. For resource types NOT listed here, derive the path from the azure2 convention: img/lib/azure2/<category>/<PascalCase_Resource_Name>.svg — but ONLY after confirming the file exists. Never guess the category from the ARM namespace: Microsoft.Web resources live in app_services/, not web/ or compute/. This file lists all known exceptions (wrong category, singular/plural surprises, non-existent files, fallback icons) and container styles.
criticalWarnings: Microsoft.Web/* resources (App Services, App Service Plans, Function Apps) live in app_services/ — NOT in compute/ or web/
App_Service_Plans.svg is in app_services/, NOT compute/. compute/ does not contain this file.
There is no generic Event_Grid.svg. Use System_Topic.svg, Event_Grid_Topics.svg, or Event_Grid_Domains.svg depending on the sub-type.

Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configurationStores uses img/lib/mscae/App_Configuration.svg — this icon does NOT exist in the azure2 library. Using any azure2 path (e.g. management_governance/Management_Groups.svg or management_governance/App_Configurations.svg) produces an empty/broken icon.
Always verify a path exists before using it. When in doubt, check https://github.com/jgraph/drawio/tree/dev/src/main/webapp/img/lib/azure2
iconNamingNotes: Most icons use plural names (e.g., Virtual_Networks.svg, Load_Balancers.svg). The exceptions below use SINGULAR names — do not pluralize them. When unsure, check the draw.io azure2 stencil source at https://github.com/jgraph/drawio/tree/dev/src/main/webapp/img/lib/azure2
iconSizingNotes: defaultWidth and defaultHeight define the mxCell bounding box. Do NOT add imageAspect=0 — it stretches non-square SVGs and distorts icons. For icons marked requiresImageAspect1=true (e.g. Application Insights), you MUST add imageAspect=1 to the style string to preserve the SVG's native non-square proportions. For all other icons, omit imageAspect entirely. Always use the exact defaultWidth and defaultHeight from this mapping; never calculate or approximate icon dimensions yourself. Most azure2 icons are square; non-square entries list an explicit defaultHeight.

resourceTypes._comment_singularExceptions: These icons use SINGULAR names, breaking the usual plural pattern
resourceTypes._comment_wrongCategory: These icons are in unexpected categories — the LLM would guess wrong

Microsoft.Web/serverFarms.label: App Service Plan
Microsoft.Web/serverFarms.note: CATEGORY: app_services — distinct icon from App_Services.svg. Do NOT use compute/ (file does not exist there).

Microsoft.Web/sites.label: App Service
Microsoft.Web/sites.note: CATEGORY: app_services — NOT compute, NOT web. The Microsoft.Web ARM namespace is misleading on both counts. For Function Apps (kind contains 'functionapp'), use the Function_Apps.svg entry below instead.

Microsoft.Web/sites[functionapp].label: Function App
Microsoft.Web/sites[functionapp].note: Use this entry when Microsoft.Web/sites has kind containing 'functionapp' (e.g. 'functionapp,linux', 'functionapp'). CATEGORY: compute — Function_Apps.svg lives in compute/, unlike App_Services.svg which is in app_services/. Always check the resource 'kind' field to distinguish Function Apps from regular App Services.

Microsoft.EventGrid/systemTopics.label: Event Grid System Topic
Microsoft.EventGrid/systemTopics.note: Use System_Topic.svg for systemTopics. There is no generic Event_Grid.svg — use Event_Grid_Topics.svg for custom topics, Event_Grid_Domains.svg for domains.

Microsoft.Communication/emailServices.label: Email Communication Services
Microsoft.Communication/emailServices.note: No distinct email icon exists in azure2 — shares ACS icon. Differentiate from communicationServices by label text (e.g., italicize or append ' (Email)' to the mxCell value).

Microsoft.Insights/components.label: Application Insights
Microsoft.Insights/components.note: The Application_Insights.svg has a non-square native ratio. You MUST include imageAspect=1 in the style string. Omitting it or using imageAspect=0 will stretch/distort the icon into a malformed square shape.

Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones.label: Private DNS Zone
Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones.note: No dedicated Private DNS icon exists — uses DNS_Zones.svg as fallback

Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configuration.label: App Configuration
Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configuration.note: LIBRARY: mscae — NOT azure2. This icon does not exist anywhere in the azure2 library. Using any azure2 path produces a broken/empty icon. The correct path is img/lib/mscae/App_Configuration.svg (singular, not plural).

resourceTypes._comment_containers: Container elements with special rendering styles
